interpretation:
DP9-W-LIFT partial computation:

(i) dP9 BBW infrastructure validated against closed-form Riemann-Roch
on 9 probe line bundles — all matched.

(ii) For each K-theoretic summand of [V_1 ⊗ V_2*] on X̃, the dP9-side
cohomology of W ⊗ L_base was computed via the BHOP §6.1 Eq. 85
SES `0 → O(-2f) → W → 2·O(2f) ⊗ I_9 → 0` with generic-position
ideal-sheaf twist for the 9 points of the Z/3×Z/3 orbit.

(iii) Total K-theoretic χ_dP9 contribution = 18

(iv) DEFINITIVE Ext¹ value not yet derivable from this computation
alone. The final answer requires the Leray spectral sequence
chase that pushes the X̃-side line bundle (with its CP²_x = τ_1
degree) forward to dP9 along π_2: X̃ → B_2; the fiber-cohomology
R^* π_{2*} of `O(a τ_1)` involves the relative dualising
sheaf of the elliptic fibration B_1 → CP¹ pulled back along
B_2 → CP¹, which is non-trivial Schoen-CY3 geometry and is
outside the scope of this single-stream binary.
open_research_items: Resolve the rank(W) = 2 (code) vs rank(W) = 3 (literal BHOP Eq. 85 reading) discrepancy. Likely the BHOP `2·O(2f)` notation means the rank-2 trivial bundle tensored with O(2f), not two copies. Cross-check against BHOP-2005 hep-th/0505041 PDF directly.

Implement R^* π_{2*} for the elliptic fibration π_2: X̃ → B_2 = dP9. The fiber over y ∈ B_2 is the cubic curve E_y ⊂ CP²_x cut by F_1(x, t(y)) = 0. For O_X̃(a, b, c)|_fiber = O_E(3a) the Künneth + relative duality gives R^q π_{2*} = (rank |3a| bundle on B_2) ⊗ O_{B_2}(b, c) for q = (a < 0 ? 1 : 0); explicit identification of the bundle requires the relative dualising sheaf of the elliptic fibration.

Push the dP9-side computation H^*(B_2, W ⊗ R^q π_{2*}(O_{X̃}(a,b,c))) through the Leray E_2 → E_∞ spectral sequence chase. Combine with the χ_dP9 results in this binary to produce h^p(X̃, V_1 ⊗ V_2*) per K-theoretic summand.

Project onto the Z/3 × Z/3 invariant subspace via character decomposition (the BBW Schoen module already does this via the Koszul chase; the analogue for the W-twisted case requires tracking Γ-equivariant W-module characters).

Resolve the BHOP V_1 rank(2) vs line-bundle expansion rank(4) discrepancy. Most likely V_1 in the paper is a non-split rank-2 extension whose K-theoretic class equals 2·[O] + 2·[O(-τ_1+τ_2)] but whose actual rank is 2; the K-theoretic Euler characteristic is the same either way, but the SES bounds on h^p will differ.
Once all of (i)-(iv) are complete: derive κ ≠ 0 and propagate through the rank-4 H¹ frame-irrep redistribution pipeline to test whether {Q, u^c, e^c} now appear at non-zero κ.
